articles.title: WORK?
articles.summary: AI isn't just a future investment or nice-to-have feature anymore. At many companies, it's now part of a top-down directive to increase productivity.

articles.title: Read the full report here ↗️
CLAUDE OPUS 4.1 LIKELY IN INTERNAL TESTING AS ANTHROPIC PREPARES SAFETY CHECKS
articles.summary: Newly surfaced references in Anthropic's configuration files suggest that it may be ramping up internal testing for what could become Claude Opus 4.1. The files hint at improved reasoning or planning capabilities. Anthropic's internal safety system is undergoing red teaming, a process that usually precedes model deployment by at least a week or two. A new Claude release would serve as Anthropic's answer to the highly anticipated GPT-5 launch, which seems imminent based on partner activity.

articles.title: XAI LAUNCHES GROK IMAGINE FOR AI VIDEO AND IMAGES
CHATGPT NEARS 700M WEEKLY USERS
articles.summary: ChatGPT is approaching 700 million weekly active users, up from 500 million in March, according to OpenAI VP Nick Turley.

articles.title: AI WILL NOT SUDDENLY LEAD TO AN ALZHEIMER'S CURE
THE RISE OF VERTICALIZED AI COWORKERS
articles.summary: AI coworkers are emerging across industries, focusing on repetitive tasks in sectors like finance, healthcare, and logistics. They integrate into existing workflows, take over specific tasks with human oversight, and are priced based on usage or outcomes. Key areas include document processing, voice-based interactions, content creation, and information retrieval.

articles.title: 📈 AI IS ELEVATING HR'S ROLE IN ENTERPRISE TECH STRATEGY
articles.summary: New research from Sapient Insights shows HR isn't just supporting tech strategy—it's driving it. Join this exclusive webinar [10] to see how HR leaders are shaping governance, ethics, and AI adoption across the enterprise.

articles.title: 👉 Reserve your spot
QWEN-IMAGE: CRAFTING WITH NATIVE TEXT RENDERING
articles.summary: Qwen-Image is a 20B MMDiT image foundation model that excels in complex text rendering and precise image rendering. It can create multi-line text layouts, paragraph-level semantics, and fine-grained details in both alphabetic languages and logographic languages with high fidelity. The model preserves both semantic meaning and visual realism during editing operations. It consistently outperforms existing models across diverse generation and editing tasks. Examples of images generated by the model are available in the post.

articles.title: IMPROVING LLM CALIBRATION WITH LABEL SMOOTHING
articles.summary: Instruction tuning degrades confidence calibration in LLMs, making outputs less reliable. This paper proposes label smoothing as a remedy and introduces a memory-efficient loss kernel, showing that calibration issues grow with vocabulary size and hidden dimensions.

articles.title: CCHISTORY: TRACKING CLAUDE CODE SYSTEM PROMPT AND TOOL CHANGES
articles.summary: A reverse-engineering project tracking Claude Code's system prompt evolution reveals Anthropic's iterative approach to AI tool development — removing emoji usage restrictions, tightening security policies, and adding PDF reading capabilities across 67 versions. The project bypasses anti-debugging measures to extract prompts, showing how AI companies continuously refine their models' behavior through detailed instruction changes rather than just model updates.

articles.title: KAGGLE GAME ARENA
articles.summary: Google has launched Kaggle Game Arena, a new open-source platform for benchmarking AI systems through direct competition in strategic games.

articles.title: RESTAURANT BOOKING WITH PERPLEXITY
articles.summary: Perplexity now integrates with OpenTable, allowing users to find and reserve restaurants directly through natural language queries. By analyzing user intent and preferences, it surfaces relevant dining options from OpenTable's network of 60,000 global partners.

META TO SHARE AI INFRASTRUCTURE COSTS VIA $2 BILLION ASSET SALE
articles.summary: Meta reclassified $2 billion in data center assets as "held-for-sale" to attract financial partners for co-development, signaling a strategic shift as tech giants seek external funding for AI infrastructure.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY ACCUSED OF IGNORING AI SCRAPING BLOCKS
articles.summary: Cloudflare claims that Perplexity bypassed robots.txt and other block signals to scrape websites that explicitly opted out.

articles.title: FINE-TUNED SMALL LLMS CAN BEAT LARGE ONES AT 5-30X LOWER COST WITH PROGRAMMATIC DATA CURATION
articles.summary: This post discusses how to reproduce the workflow using open-source LLMOps tools, even without a GPU, as well as best practices for production deployments.

articles.title: WHY TECH IS RACING TO ADOPT AI CODING
articles.summary: An interview with Michael Truell, CEO of Anysphere, the maker of Cursor, an automated programming platform that integrates with generated AI models to help developers code.
